findings.title: The global uniqueness claim fails at the equality boundary
findings.evidence_boundary: No tie-breaking rule or boundary restriction appears in the supplied manuscript.
findings.minimum_repair: Add a tie-breaking rule or state a set-valued equilibrium at the boundary.
findings.reader_effect: The proposition and comparative-static summary currently state a stronger global conclusion.
findings.issue: The proposition asserts strict uniqueness although the stated payoff permits a tie.
findings.why_it_matters: At equality both actions maximize payoff, so the model supports a set-valued prediction.

evidence.content: The equilibrium action is unique for every parameter value.

confidence.would_change_my_mind: A stated tie-breaking rule or a proof that the equality case is outside the parameter domain.

counterargument.author_reply: The equality case may be ruled out implicitly by the parameter restriction.
counterargument.search_scope: Assumptions 1-3, Proposition 1, its proof, and the available appendix text.
counterargument.notes: The stated domain includes the equality case and no tie-breaking rule appears.

fix.what: Add a tie-breaking rule or state a set-valued equilibrium at the boundary.
fix.how: Align Proposition 1, its proof, and the comparative static.

patch.after: unique away from the boundary; at equality both actions are optimal

fix.publishability: Preempts a direct logical objection to the headline proposition.
fix.claim_narrowing_alternative: Limit the proposition to parameter values away from the equality boundary.
fix.resolved_when: Proposition 1 and its proof either supply a tie-breaking rule or state set-valued equilibrium at equality, and the comparative-static summary uses the same boundary qualification.

findings.title: The proposition summary has a subject-verb agreement error
findings.evidence_boundary: Only this occurrence was found in the supplied manuscript.
findings.reader_effect: The surrounding prose is otherwise concise and readable.
findings.issue: A singular subject takes a plural verb in the proposition summary.
findings.why_it_matters: The error appears in the sentence summarizing the main proposition, making a simple copyedit unusually visible.

evidence.content: The proposition characterize the model's comparative static.

confidence.would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the intended grammatical subject is plural rather than the singular noun 'proposition'.

counterargument.author_reply: The sentence may have been intended to refer to multiple results rather than one proposition.
counterargument.search_scope: The complete sentence, the surrounding paragraph, and the statement of Proposition 1.
counterargument.notes: Objective source-verified subject-verb agreement correction; no substantive counterargument is applicable.

patch.before: The proposition characterize the model's comparative static.
patch.after: The proposition characterizes the model's comparative static.

fix.publishability: Removes an objective grammar error from the proposition summary.
fix.resolved_when: The proposition summary uses 'characterizes' with the singular subject and no duplicate occurrence remains.
